title: getLastElement returns undefined for non-empty arrays
body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open `src/utils.ts`.
2. Call `getLastElement([1, 2, 3])`.
3. The function returns `undefined` instead of `3` because it reads `arr[arr.length]`.

Expected behavior:
Return the final array element for any non-empty numeric array.

title: getStringLength throws when the input is null
body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open `src/utils.ts`.
2. Call `getStringLength(null)`.
3. The function throws immediately because it reads `.length` from a null value without guarding first.

Expected behavior:
Return a safe fallback such as `0` when the input string is missing.

title: isEqual always returns true for most non-zero comparisons
body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Open `src/utils.ts`.
2. Call `isEqual(3, 7)`.
3. The function returns `true` because the condition assigns `b` into `a` instead of comparing both values.

Expected behavior:
Only return `true` when the two numeric inputs are actually equal.
